old_claim.note: Basic Fermentation is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The identified product was curdled by a symbiotic culture of lactic-acid bacteria and yeasts, so microbial fermentation is constitutive of the scoped kefir dairy.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Discussion attribute curdling to Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens together with other lactic-acid bacteria and yeasts.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: A symbiotic microbial culture fermented the ruminant milk into the identified kefir cheese.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fermentation defines the recovered product and distinguishes it from rennet curdling or untreated milk, so it is part of the method itself.

ontology_before: Fermentation was treated only as a broad historical background for an unspecified dairy category.
ontology_after: The source-pinned product directly requires the microbial process identified by proteomics.
would_reject_if: The microbial proteins or process markers were shown not to represent kefir fermentation of the Xiaohe dairy masses.
short_reason: Microbial kefir fermentation is the defining production process of the recovered cheese.
